title: BuildMonitorService creates its own BambooApiClient that becomes stale after Bamboo URL changes
description: BuildMonitorService owns a second BambooApiClient (lines 47–58) that is created once from settings and cached as `_apiClient`. Unlike BambooServiceImpl.client (which invalidates when bambooUrl changes via `cachedBaseUrl` comparison), BuildMonitorService never re-checks the URL. If the user updates the Bamboo URL in settings, the monitor keeps polling the old host until the IDE restarts. This also means there are three independent BambooApiClient instances per project (BambooServiceImpl, BuildMonitorService, BuildFailureBridgeStartupActivity per-call), each with their own OkHttp connection pool, instead of one shared pool.
suggestedFix: Remove BuildMonitorService's private BambooApiClient entirely. Inject BambooServiceImpl (or its internal `client` getter) into BuildMonitorService so all Bamboo HTTP calls in the module share the single URL-change-aware client from BambooServiceImpl. The cleanest form: add an internal `getPollClient(): BambooApiClient?` method on BambooServiceImpl (delegating to its existing `client` getter) and call that from BuildMonitorService instead of constructing its own client.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed real: `BuildMonitorService._apiClient` (lines 47–58) is constructed once with the URL from settings and cached forever (`.also { _apiClient = it }`). Unlike `BambooServiceImpl.client`, there is no `cachedBaseUrl` comparison that would rebuild the client on URL change. A user changing the Bamboo URL in settings would need to restart the IDE for `BuildMonitorService` to pick it up. That said, severity is Low not Medium: (1) URL changes are rare, expected to require a restart by most users; (2) the test constructor explicitly sets `_apiClient` directly (line 93–100), confirming the caching is intentional for that seam. The third-client issue (alongside BambooServiceImpl and BuildFailureBridgeStartupActivity) is a maintenance concern. Fix is not harmful: delegating to `BambooServiceImpl.client` via a `getPollClient()` method keeps tests working via the test-constructor seam.
recommendedAction: Add `internal fun getPollClient(): BambooApiClient? = client` on BambooServiceImpl and replace BuildMonitorService's private `_apiClient`/`apiClient` with calls to `project.getService(BambooServiceImpl::class.java)?.getPollClient()`. Keep the test constructor's explicit `apiClient` param as-is.

title: Dead public method: BuildMonitorService.switchBranch() has no callers
description: The public `switchBranch(planKey, newBranch, intervalMs)` method (line 203) is referenced only in comments in BuildDashboardPanel.kt (twice, explaining it is no longer used). The T-B2/B3-b migration removed all direct `switchBranch` call sites; the KDoc in BuildDashboardPanelDropdownTest explicitly states 'switchBranch/stopPolling are absent from the new dropdown path'. No caller outside the bamboo module exists either. The method simply calls `_stateFlow.value = null; startPolling(...)` — callers now go through the focusBuild subscription instead.
suggestedFix: Delete the `switchBranch()` method (lines 203–207). Its only remaining references are comments documenting that it has been removed. `startPolling` is still reachable from the focusBuild subscription path.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. `BuildMonitorService.switchBranch()` at line 203 has no call sites in production code. All references in BuildDashboardPanel.kt (lines 266, 278, 454, 487, 656, 734) are comments explicitly documenting that the method is NO LONGER called, consistent with the T-B2/B3-b migration. The CLAUDE.md module notes for bamboo also document 'BuildDashboardPanel no longer calls startPolling, switchBranch, or stopPolling directly — all three were removed.' The test file (BuildDashboardPanelDropdownTest.kt lines 15, 22, 38, 210) references it only in KDoc comments confirming its absence. Deleting the 5-line method is safe.
recommendedAction: Delete lines 203–207 (the `switchBranch` method body and its opening/closing braces).

title: Dead DTO field: BambooTestResultsDto.quarantined is deserialized but never read
description: The `quarantined: Int = 0` field at line 307 in `BambooTestResultsDto` is deserialized from the Bamboo JSON response but never referenced in any production code. The fields `all`, `successful`, `failed`, `skipped`, `failedTests`, and `successfulTests` are all used; `quarantined` is not. No agent tool, service mapper, or UI panel reads it.
evidence:
307:    val quarantined: Int = 0,
// rg '\.quarantined\b' --include='*.kt' src/ returns zero results
suggestedFix: Delete the `quarantined` field from `BambooTestResultsDto`. If Bamboo quarantine data is needed in the future it can be re-added with an explicit consumer.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. `BambooTestResultsDto.quarantined: Int = 0` at line 307 is deserialized from JSON but never accessed anywhere in production code. A project-wide grep returns zero `.quarantined` reads in main/ sources. Removing the field causes kotlinx.serialization to silently ignore the JSON key if Bamboo returns it — this is the correct behavior and carries no risk.

title: Dead DTO field: BambooJobResultDto.buildDuration is deserialized but never read
description: The `buildDuration: Long = 0` field at line 139 in `BambooJobResultDto` duplicates `buildDurationInSeconds: Long = 0` (line 138). All mappers in `BambooBuildStructureMapper.toBuildState()` and `BambooServiceImpl.toBuildStageData()` use `buildDurationInSeconds`; `buildDuration` is never referenced.
suggestedFix: Delete the `buildDuration` field from `BambooJobResultDto`. It is a Bamboo API artifact (the API returns both milliseconds and seconds representations) that the codebase never uses.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. `BambooJobResultDto.buildDuration: Long = 0` at line 139 duplicates `buildDurationInSeconds: Long = 0` at line 138. All mappers (`BambooBuildStructureMapper.toBuildState()` and `BambooServiceImpl.toBuildStageData()`) consume `buildDurationInSeconds`; zero reads of `.buildDuration` appear in main/ sources. The Bamboo API returning both millisecond and second representations is expected but the millisecond field is unused.

title: Dead DTO field: BambooPlanDto.shortKey is deserialized but never read
description: The `shortKey: String = ""` field at line 51 in `BambooPlanDto` is deserialized but never accessed in any production or test file. The fields actually consumed are `key`, `name`, `shortName`, `enabled`, `type`, and `projectKey`.
evidence:
51:    val shortKey: String = "",
// rg '\.shortKey\b' --include='*.kt' src/ returns zero results
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. `BambooPlanDto.shortKey: String = ""` at line 51 is deserialized but never accessed in production code — zero `.shortKey` reads in main/ sources. Test files (BambooApiClientPaginationTest.kt, BambooPlanJobOrderTest.kt, BambooServiceImplGetPlansProjectKeyTest.kt) include it only as fixture JSON or constructor arguments for building test DTOs; none read the field back from a mapped object. Removal is safe.

title: Pervasive dead DTO pagination metadata fields: size/maxResult/startIndex on collection wrappers are never read
description: Every Bamboo paginated collection wrapper DTO declares `size: Int`, `maxResult: Int`, and/or `startIndex: Int` to mirror the Bamboo JSON envelope. None of these fields are ever read by production code — only the inner list field (`.plan`, `.branch`, `.result`, `.variable`, `.stage`, etc.) is accessed. Affected wrappers: BambooProjectCollection (line 15 size), BambooPlanCollection (lines 42-44), BambooBranchCollection (lines 74-76), BambooResultCollection (line 92), BambooStageCollection (line 112), BambooJobResultCollection (line 128), BambooVcsRevisionCollection (line 157), BambooVariableContextCollection (line 194), BambooVariableCollection (line 213), BambooBuildChangeCollection (line 237), BambooSearchResponse (lines 259-261), BambooTestCaseCollection (line 314), BambooChangesetResultList (line 375), BambooLinkedRepositoryListResponse (lines 399-401). The `paginate()` loop in BambooApiClient explicitly documents it does NOT trust the server-echoed `startIndex`/`size` — it uses `items.size` (the Kotlin list size) instead.
evidence:
42:data class BambooPlanCollection(
43:    val size: Int = 0,
44:    @SerialName("max-result") val maxResult: Int = 25,
45:    @SerialName("start-index") val startIndex: Int = 0,
46:    val plan: List<BambooPlanDto> = emptyList()
// BambooApiClient.paginate() comment: "never from the server-echoed start-index/size fields"
// rg produces zero results for any read of these fields in production code
suggestedFix: Delete all `size`, `maxResult`, and `startIndex` fields from the collection wrapper DTOs. The `paginate()` loop uses `items.size` (Kotlin list length) for cursor advancement and termination, not these server-echoed values. The kotlinx.serialization `@SerialName` fields will simply be ignored during deserialization if the JSON contains them — removing the declared field is safe.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. The `paginate()` loop at BambooApiClient.kt lines 624–651 explicitly derives cursor advancement from `items.size` (the Kotlin list count of actually-returned items) and never reads the DTO-level `size`, `maxResult`, or `startIndex` fields. The method's KDoc (lines 612–614) documents this intentional design. All affected collection wrappers (BambooPlanCollection, BambooBranchCollection, BambooResultCollection, BambooStageCollection, BambooJobResultCollection, BambooVcsRevisionCollection, BambooVariableContextCollection, BambooVariableCollection, BambooBuildChangeCollection, BambooSearchResponse, BambooTestCaseCollection, BambooChangesetResultList, BambooLinkedRepositoryListResponse, BambooPlanBranchCollection) have these fields deserialized but zero production reads. Note: `BambooArtifact.size: Long` (line 358) is genuinely used (line BambooServiceImpl.kt:497) but is a file-size field on a non-collection DTO and is not part of this finding.
recommendedAction: Delete all `size: Int`, `maxResult: Int`, and `startIndex: Int` fields from the listed collection wrapper DTOs. The `paginate()` loop correctly ignores them and uses the Kotlin list length for cursor arithmetic.

title: Dead DTO fields: BambooProjectDetailResponse.key and .name are never accessed
description: BambooProjectDetailResponse (line 27) declares `key: String = ""` and `name: String = ""` in addition to `plans: BambooPlanCollection`. The only caller `getProjectPlans()` in BambooApiClient.kt maps with `.map { it.plans.plan }`, so `key` and `name` are deserialized but immediately discarded. No other code path reads them.
suggestedFix: Delete the `key` and `name` fields from `BambooProjectDetailResponse`. If the response envelope's project key or name is needed by a future caller, they can be re-added then.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. `BambooProjectDetailResponse.key: String = ""` and `.name: String = ""` at lines 28–29 are deserialized but immediately discarded. The sole caller `getProjectPlans()` at BambooApiClient.kt line 56–57 maps `.map { it.plans.plan }`, accessing only `it.plans`. No other code path accesses these two fields. Removing them is safe; kotlinx.serialization will silently ignore the JSON keys.
recommendedAction: Delete the `key: String = ""` and `name: String = ""` fields (lines 28–29) from `BambooProjectDetailResponse`.
